perturbation.original_text: Upon transition to the third phase - Production, the Contractor will be responsible for all purchasing of components and getting equipped with all the materials necessary for the assembly of the Products (except the Non Generic Equipment). At NICE' request, and without derogating from any other provisions of this Agreement, Contractor shall promptly notify NICE, in writing, who are the suppliers of any specific components and under what agreements purchase is effected.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon transition to the third phase - Production, NICE will oversee and approve all purchasing of components, ensuring alignment with budget and strategic vendor relationships. At NICE's discretion, Contractor may be involved in the selection of suppliers for specific components, subject to NICE's final approval and contractual agreements. NICE shall promptly notify the contractor, in writing, which supplier and agreement to purchase from.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the 'Contractor will be responsible for all purchasing of components,' while the modified text states that 'NICE will oversee and approve all purchasing of components.' This creates an in-text contradiction, where section 2.1 states Contractor will be responsible for all purchasing while the modified text clearly changes the responsibility to NICE. Uncertainty is introduced regarding which party has ultimate control over component purchasing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPONENTS PURCHASING. Upon transition to the third phase - Production, NICE will oversee and approve all purchasing of components, ensuring alignment with budget and strategic vendor relationships. At NICE's discretion, Contractor may be involved in the selection of suppliers for specific components, subject to NICE's final approval and contractual agreements. NICE shall promptly notify the contractor, in writing, which supplier and agreement to purchase from.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigns the responsibility of 'all purchasing of components' to the Contractor. The changed text explicitly shifts this responsibility to NICE, stating 'NICE will oversee and approve all purchasing of components.' The contradicted text restates the changed text, reinforcing NICE's control over purchasing. This directly contradicts the original allocation of responsibility and creates a significant in-text contradiction as per the structural flaws definition, making it unclear who is ultimately in charge of purchasing.

perturbation.original_text: NICE shall give Contractor a written forecast of the Products, by type, quantity and expected Due Date (the "FORECAST") as follows: A Forecast shall be a rolling forecast for the current Quarter and the two (2) consecutive Quarters, detailed by week. Upon the Effective Date, NICE shall deliver a Forecast for the period commencing on the expected completion of the Relocation Phase for the first Product, for the current Quarter and for the next coming two Quarters and so on thereafter during the term of this Agreement. The Forecast may be updated by NICE on a weekly basis, or more frequently (at NICE' discretion). A Forecast will not be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order with respect to Products (as opposed to components and sub-assemblies, as set forth in Sections 3.4, 16 and 17.5 of this Agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: NICE shall give Contractor a written forecast of the Products, by type, quantity and expected Due Date (the "FORECAST") as follows: A Forecast shall be a rolling forecast for the current Quarter and the two (2) consecutive Quarters, detailed by week. Upon the Effective Date, NICE shall deliver a Forecast for the period commencing on the expected completion of the Relocation Phase for the first Product, for the current Quarter and for the next coming two Quarters and so on thereafter during the term of this Agreement. The Forecast may be updated by NICE on a weekly basis, or more frequently (at NICE' discretion). A Forecast will be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order with respect to Products. NICE is obligated to purchase products based on the forecast.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the 'Forecast will not be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order with respect to Products', while the modified text directly contradicts this by stating 'A Forecast will be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order with respect to Products. NICE is obligated to purchase products based on the forecast.' This introduces a contradiction about whether NICE is obligated to purchase based on the forecast.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FORECASTS. NICE shall give Contractor a written forecast of the Products, by type, quantity and expected Due Date (the "FORECAST") as follows: A Forecast shall be a rolling forecast for the current Quarter and the two (2) consecutive Quarters, detailed by week. Upon the Effective Date, NICE shall deliver a Forecast for the period commencing on the expected completion of the Relocation Phase for the first Product, for the current Quarter and for the next coming two Quarters and so on thereafter during the term of this Agreement. The Forecast may be updated by NICE on a weekly basis, or more frequently (at NICE' discretion). A Forecast will be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order with respect to Products. NICE is obligated to purchase products based on the forecast.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text by stating that the forecast 'will be deemed to constitute a binding purchase order' whereas the original and contradicted text indicates that it will NOT be deemed a binding purchase order. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract unenforceable because of the opposing positions regarding NICE's obligation to purchase.

perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and extend for three (3) years thereafter ("INITIAL TERM"), with an automatic renewal for an indefinite period of time ("EXTENDED TERM"), unless terminated by the parties according to Sections 16.2. or 16.3. herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and extend for three (3) years thereafter ("INITIAL TERM"), without any renewal period whatsoever and the agreement shall be considered over and no extended term whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies an 'automatic renewal for an indefinite period of time' after the initial term, while the modified text removes the renewal period altogether, stating there will be 'no extended term whatsoever.' This contradiction creates uncertainty about the contract's duration and renewal options. This changes and makes 16.1 contradict with 16.2 and 16.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM AND TERMINATION. The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and extend for three (3) years thereafter ("INITIAL TERM"), without any renewal period whatsoever and the agreement shall be considered over and no extended term whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes the automatic renewal period, stating 'no extended term whatsoever.' The contradicted text, which is strangely the same as the changed text (implying this is a replacement), also states 'no extended term whatsoever.' However, the explanation states that this change contradicts sections 16.2 and 16.3, which, based on the original text, presumably outline termination conditions related to the extended term. By removing the extended term in the changed text, and by extension, in the contradicted text (as it's the same), it fundamentally conflicts with the provisions of sections 16.2 and 16.3, which would become nonsensical as they pertain to something that no longer exists. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is created.
